headline: Non-Muslim Cabinet Representation Falls to 18% from 28% in 2008
context: Non-Muslim representation in the federal Cabinet fell from 28% in 2008 to 18% in the current administration, the lowest since independence.

cards.sub: They hold 18% of Cabinet seats — the lowest since 1957. Representation has halved in relative terms.

cards.big: No non-Muslim has held the Finance, Defence, Home, or Education portfolio since 1974.
cards.sub: The 4 most powerful ministries have been exclusively Muslim for 52 years.

cards.big: Article 8(1) guarantees equality before the law.
cards.sub: No provision restricts Cabinet composition by religion. The exclusion is political practice, not constitutional requirement.

cards.big: India reserved the presidency for a Dalit (2017) and tribal woman (2022).
cards.sub: Malaysian political norms move in the opposite direction — narrowing, not broadening, representation.

cards.big: 38.7% of the population, 18% of the Cabinet. The gap is not accidental — it is structural.
Representative governance requires that those who govern look like those they govern. 18% Cabinet representation for 38.7% of the population is a democratic deficit.
